message:
test(ft-conformance): lock torch fmod/remainder sign-matrix parity

torch.fmod uses truncating modulo (sign of dividend, like C's fmod) and
torch.remainder uses flooring modulo (sign of divisor, like Python's
% operator and numpy.remainder). The two are trivially easy to swap by
accident — and the previous ft-api smoke tests only covered (positive,
positive) and (negative, positive) lhs cases. This adds a conformance
harness that locks the full sign matrix plus zero-divisor NaN behavior:

    lhs   rhs   fmod   remainder
     7     3      1        1
    -7     3     -1        2
     7    -3      1       -2
    -7    -3     -1       -1
     6     3      0        0
    -6     3     -0        0
     3.5   1.5   0.5      0.5
    -3.5   1.5  -0.5      1.0
     3.5  -1.5   0.5     -1.0
    -3.5  -1.5  -0.5     -0.5
     0     5      0        0

Plus: torch_fmod_remainder_zero_divisor_returns_nan documents that
both ops yield NaN (not panic, not 0) when divisor is zero — matching
torch.fmod / torch.remainder.

ft-conformance: 93 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
